title: podman (docker-compose) incompatible with python v3.8.
body:
/kind bug

Hate to be a bother, but...
Did a system upgrade today.  Then:

# zypper search docker-compose
Loading repository data...
Reading installed packages...

S  | Name                    | Summary                                                  | Type
---+-------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------+--------
   | python36-docker-compose | Tool to define and run complex applications using Docker | package
i+ | python38-docker-compose | Tool to define and run complex applications using Docker | package


# docker-compose up -d
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 583, in _build_master
    ws.require(__requires__)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 900, in require
    needed = self.resolve(parse_requirements(requirements))
  File "/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 791, in resolve
    raise VersionConflict(dist, req).with_context(dependent_req)
pkg_resources.VersionConflict: (docker-compose 1.28.5 (/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages), Requirement.parse('docker-compose==1.26.2'))

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/bill/bin/docker-compose", line 6, in <module>
    from pkg_resources import load_entry_point
  File "/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3252, in <module>
    def _initialize_master_working_set():
  File "/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3235, in _call_aside
    f(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3264, in _initialize_master_working_set
    working_set = WorkingSet._build_master()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 585, in _build_master
    return cls._build_from_requirements(__requires__)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 598, in _build_from_requirements
    dists = ws.resolve(reqs, Environment())
  File "/usr/lib/python3.8/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 786, in resolve
    raise DistributionNotFound(req, requirers)
pkg_resources.DistributionNotFound: The 'docker-compose==1.26.2' distribution was not found and is required by the application

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out and apologies for the longer silence on this issue; we've been quite busy.

Please follow the requested issue template for reporting issues. Using zypper suggests you're on OpenSUSE but we really need more information about the host environment _and_ a clear reproducer.

I no longer remember the context this was in as I've been through many podman Hells since, but I suspect I was trying to stand up mailu/postfix.

All I can say is here's what I see now:

`quantumn:~/mailu$ dir /run/docker.sock
lrwxrwxrwx  1 root   root     23 Mar 31 07:09 docker.sock -> /run/podman/podman.sock

quantumn:~/mailu$ dir /run/podman/
total 0
drwxrwxrwx  2 root root  60 Mar 31 07:09 .
drwxr-xr-x 28 root root 780 Mar 31 07:18 ..
srwxrwxrwx  1 root root   0 Mar 31 07:09 podman.sock

quantumn:~/mailu$ docker-compose up -d
Starting mailu_resolver_1 ... done
Starting mailu_front_1     ... done
Starting mailu_webdav_1   ... done
Starting mailu_redis_1    ... done
Starting mailu_admin_1     ... done
Starting mailu_antivirus_1 ... done
Starting mailu_fetchmail_1 ... done
Starting mailu_antispam_1  ... done
Starting mailu_imap_1      ... done
Starting mailu_smtp_1      ... done

quantumn:~/mailu$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES

quantumn:~/mailu$ podman pod ps
^C
quantumn:~/mailu$ podman log -l`
^C

Yes it just hangs on any podman command.  I've since moved on to trying to construct my wireguard and dns transactional server, but can't even get the simple docker.io/mvance/unbound:latest daemon working in a rootless pod.  --volume has billions of access denied for permissions.  It is able to create the unbound.pid with subuid, but everything else in the directory gives permissions errors.  Yes I'd had everything in that directory set to my user, and yes I'd tried setting everything to the subuid, and no selinux and apparmor are not in the picture.  Nothing works.  MVance tried earnestly to help but he just doesn't know the ins and outs of pods, rootless, and podman.  He's a docker type.

Shelling inside the container the mounted volume is accessible and writeable, and when permissions set to the subuid, -properly- shows within the container to be owned by _unbound.  But the container's unbound daemon can not access?!  WTF?  In the podman run line I even tried  -e PUID=1003 -e PGID=1002 of my user, but no dice.

The only virtual machine I've been able to make run is my Deluge one, and that's with no pod and --net=host which is damned dangerous.  Clearly I'm a power user pushing this beyond a simple ngnix server to the functions it is ostensibly supposed to have, but I suspect that no one is actually testing real-world.  Can't be.

My old OpenStack email system is barely running and my old webserver system is barely running, and my old wireguard/DNS transactional server is practically dead as I've fiddled around with this nonsense for weeks!   What am I supposed to think, especially with the hostile reception I've gotten? (And intentional silence is hostile aggression)

In desperation my last resort now is to create an empty pod and container and hand-build my own app inside each.  That will take quite a bit of study I suspect, and I have a state Supreme Court brief to finish, but if I can't make it work that way I've gotta ditch podman for a couple years until it's ready.

It's not worth my spending the time to document these anymore as my words don't make any sense to those who can do something about these issues.  Maybe English is not their native language, or maybe they're swamped in minutia, Idk.  I've tried and tried to explain and politely ask for guidance, but we just live in different universes apparently.
Did you do the `podman pod ps` etc. as rootless user? Something funky must be going on then, maybe unrelated, since `docker-compose` is talking to the rootful Podman.

Right, I believe the directions show to do docker-compose up -d as root;  that the transition won't work rootless yet.  But I've tried it that way 250 times.  I'll do it now:

`quantumn:/home/bill/mailu# docker-compose up -d
Starting mailu_front_1    ... error
Starting mailu_resolver_1 ... 
Starting mailu_redis_1    ... 
Starting mailu_webdav_1   ... 
Starting mailu_resolver_1 ... done
Starting mailu_redis_1     ... done
Starting mailu_webdav_1   ... done
Starting mailu_fetchmail_1 ... 
Starting mailu_antivirus_1 ... 
Starting mailu_admin_1     ... error
Starting mailu_fetchmail_1 ... done
Starting mailu_antivirus_1 ... done
586f56e4082: failed to make veth pair: operation not permitted

ERROR: for front  Cannot start service front: error configuring network namespace for container f7110b770c82d19db9409032dfa571921e6d7ac1a99606858131372a136051a4: failed to make veth pair: operation not permitted

ERROR: for admin  Cannot start service admin: error configuring network namespace for container 987dd3744e083878197c5dc78167754e840e35bc9ad94394227ca586f56e4082: failed to make veth pair: operation not permitted
ERROR: Encountered errors while bringing up the project.`

And certainly  I know better than to try and switch between root and user when trying to stand up and check on a pod/container/image, as you can see above. ($)  In this circumstance about the only thing I use root for is changing permissions and checking listening ports. (# lsof -i -n -w -P)

I was devastated when I couldn't even get simple unbound running.  That is the moment I realized, "It's not me.  It's you."
Are you using docker-compose or podman-compose?

Duh, docker-compose. There is no podman-compose.

Who do you think I am?

‐‐‐‐‐‐‐ Original Message ‐‐‐‐‐‐‐
On Wednesday, March 31, 2021 2:09 PM, Daniel J Walsh ***@***.***> wrote:

> Are you using docker-compose or podman-compose?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.
> Reply to this email directly, [view it on GitHub](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9819#issuecomment-811467140), or [unsubscribe](https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AIQXCSVJHTG2VD7CB2BTGJDTGOFRTANCNFSM4ZZ26ERA).
Please remain polite if you want us to resolve the issue.

Don't you think that was a misleading and unfair question V?  Are we going to be rational and honest, or not?

Anyway I've given up believing that this can/will be resolved within a reasonable time;  that is an amount of time where I will be able to use it.

Podman-compose is a thing, that works with docker compose files and translates them to a podman commands.

So duh, it was a valid question.

https://github.com/containers/podman-compose
Please be advised that that project is shut down and obsolete.  However fair enough;  I had forgotten about it and was unfair to you.  Apologies.
Go ahead and close my other bugs too, since you're not interested.
